name: The queen of Sheba
notes: The queen of Sheba hears of Solomon's fame, comes to prove him with hard questions, and is overwhelmed by his wisdom and his house until there was no more spirit in her. The Kings and Chronicles accounts run almost word for word, from her great train of camels bearing spices and gold, to her confession that the half was not told her, her gift of 120 talents, the navy and almug (algum) trees, Solomon's yearly 666 talents of gold, the targets and shields of beaten gold, the great ivory throne with six steps and twelve lions, the gold drinking vessels, and silver made as stones. Where wording shifts, the two stand as complementary vantages: Kings names the name of the LORD in her hearing and the navy of Hiram from Ophir, while Chronicles names the servants of Huram and adds that Solomon reigned over all the kings from the river to the border of Egypt. Small differences in the shields' shekels and the count of stalls or chariots are left as a matter for further study.

links.label: the queen of Sheba heard of the fame of Solomon ... she came to prove him with hard questions
camels that bare spices ... gold ... and precious stones ... she communed with him of all that was in her heart
Solomon told her all her questions ... not any thing hid
had seen ... wisdom ... and the house that he had built
the meat of his table and the sitting of his servants ... his ascent by which he went up ... no more spirit in her
It was a true report ... of thy acts and of thy wisdom
I believed not ... until I came and mine eyes had seen it ... the half was not told me
Happy are thy men ... these thy servants which stand continually before thee ... thy wisdom
Blessed be the LORD thy God which delighted in thee ... loved Israel ... to do judgment and justice
she gave the king an hundred and twenty talents of gold and of spices ... and precious stones
brought gold from Ophir ... almug trees and precious stones
the king made of the almug trees ... harps also and psalteries for singers
Solomon gave ... all her desire whatsoever she asked ... she turned and went to her own country she and her servants
the weight of gold that came to Solomon in one year was six hundred threescore and six talents
all the kings of Arabia and ... the governors of the country
two hundred targets of beaten gold six hundred shekels of gold went to one target
three hundred shields of beaten gold ... the king put them in the house of the forest of Lebanon
the king made a great throne of ivory and overlaid it with ... gold
six steps ... stays ... two lions stood beside the stays
twelve lions stood there ... upon the six steps there was not the like made in any kingdom
all ... drinking vessels were of gold ... none were of silver it was nothing accounted of in the days of Solomon
once in three years came the navy ... bringing gold and silver ivory and apes and peacocks
Solomon exceeded all the kings of the earth for riches and for wisdom
sought to ... Solomon to hear his wisdom which God had put in his heart
every man his present vessels of silver and vessels of gold ... spices horses and mules a rate year by year
twelve thousand horsemen whom he bestowed in the ... cities ... and with the king at Jerusalem
the king made silver ... in Jerusalem as stones and cedars ... as the sycomore trees that are in the vale
horses brought out of Egypt
a chariot ... out of Egypt
